backlog.title: Two-axis key subsystem (discovery + options + binding)
backlog.description: Replace the repurposed single-axis key model (window-nav keys → session-nav) with two discovered navigation axes: a window axis (discovered window-nav keys flip the previewed session's windows) and a session axis (discovered switch-client -n/-p keys + arrows move between sessions). Both axes are discovered from the user's live key tables. The new bindings are inert until P2 implements the preview/flip/confirm actions.

milestones.title: Options + discovery helper
milestones.description: Replace the 4 old single-axis nav option accessors with 4 new two-axis accessors (empty defaults → discovery fills them), and implement the lp_discover_axis_keys helper that scans list-keys to classify and collect keys per axis+direction.

tasks.title: Replace 4 nav options + remove old ones
tasks.description: Delete opt_next_key/opt_prev_key/opt_nav_next_keys/opt_nav_prev_keys from options.sh. Add opt_session_next_keys/opt_session_prev_keys/opt_window_next_keys/opt_window_prev_keys, each with an EMPTY default so an unset option means 'run discovery'. A non-empty user-set value overrides discovery for that axis.

subtasks.title: Replace 4 old nav accessors with 4 new two-axis accessors in options.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_key_discovery.md and system_context.md §'Confirmed user key bindings'. The current options.sh:30-33 defines 4 accessors: opt_next_key (@livepicker-next-key default C-M-Tab), opt_prev_key (@livepicker-prev-key default C-M-BTab), opt_nav_next_keys (@livepicker-nav-next-keys default Down), opt_nav_prev_keys (@livepicker-nav-prev-keys default Up). ALL are session-nav. The PRD §11 table (h2.11) names them @livepicker-session-next-keys/-session-prev-keys/@livepicker-window-next-keys/-window-prev-keys with (discovered) defaults.
2. INPUT: options.sh exists with get_opt() helper at line 5. The 4 old accessors are at lines 30-33.
3. LOGIC: Delete the 4 old accessors. Add 4 new accessors, each get_opt "@livepicker-<suffix>" "" with EMPTY string default (so unset = discover). The accessors: opt_session_next_keys() → @livepicker-session-next-keys, opt_session_prev_keys() → @livepicker-session-prev-keys, opt_window_next_keys() → @livepicker-window-next-keys, opt_window_prev_keys() → @livepicker-window-prev-keys. Each returns a SPACE-SEPARATED list (caller word-splits). The empty default is intentional — activate (P1.M2.T1) distinguishes 'explicitly set' (non-empty → use directly) from 'discover' (empty → run lp_discover_axis_keys). Do NOT add discovery logic here — discovery lives in utils.sh (P1.M1.T2).
4. OUTPUT: 4 new accessors in options.sh that consumers (livepicker.sh T4 block) call to get the resolved key list per axis. Old accessors MUST be removed (not commented out) so any stale reference is caught at runtime.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md Configuration table — remove the 4 old option rows (@livepicker-next-key, -prev-key, -nav-next-keys, -nav-prev-keys) and add 4 new rows (@livepicker-session-next-keys, -session-prev-keys, -window-next-keys, -window-prev-keys) noting the (discovered) defaults and the 'must be non-alphanumeric' constraint from PRD §8.

tasks.title: lp_discover_axis_keys helper (utils.sh)
tasks.description: Add a helper lp_discover_axis_keys <axis> <direction> that scans tmux list-keys -T root and -T prefix, classifies each key by its top-level command token, drops plain letters/digits and mouse keys, de-duplicates, and excludes fixed control keys. Prints the de-duplicated space-separated key list for the requested axis+direction.

subtasks.title: Implement lp_discover_axis_keys discovery algorithm in utils.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/external_deps.md §2 'Key Discovery' and system_context.md §'Confirmed user key bindings'. CRITICAL PARSING DETAILS: (a) The bind-key line format is 'bind-key [-r] -T <table> <key> <command...>' — the optional -r flag shifts positions, so parse the key as the token AFTER '-T <table>' (skipping -r if present). (b) Mouse bindings (MouseDown3Status, WheelUpStatus, WheelDownStatus, M-MouseDown3StatusLeft) contain 'switch-client -n'/'next-window'/'select-window -n' INSIDE display-menu blocks — these are NOT keyboard nav. Discovery MUST match only the TOP-LEVEL command token immediately following the key spec, NOT a loose substring over the whole line, AND exclude keys matching Mouse*/Wheel*. (c) The swap-window compound pattern: 'C-M-Tab → swap-window -t +1 \; select-window -t +1' — discovery matches 'select-window -t +1' (and '-t -1') in the command portion, catching the compound after the \;.
2. INPUT: utils.sh is sourced by options.sh, livepicker.sh, input-handler.sh, preview.sh, restore.sh. Existing helpers: lp_filter_harmful_bindings (line 102), lp_resolve_client (line 157), lp_client_format (line 172), tmux_is_set (line 65), tmux_get_hook/tmux_clear_hook. The function receives $1=axis (window|session) and $2=direction (next|prev).
3. LOGIC: Implement lp_discover_axis_keys AXIS DIR:
   a. Read tmux list-keys -T root AND tmux list-keys -T prefix into a combined stream.
   b. For each line, PARSE the key token: after 'bind-key' skip optional '-r', skip '-T <table>', the next token is the key. Skip keys matching Mouse* or Wheel*.
   c. Parse the command portion (everything after the key). Classify by axis+direction:
      - window/next: command contains 'select-window -n' OR 'select-window -t +1' OR 'select-window -t :+1' OR 'next-window' (incl 'next-window -a').
      - window/prev: command contains 'select-window -p' OR 'select-window -t -1' OR 'select-window -t :-1' OR 'previous-window'.
      - session/next: command matches 'switch-client -n' (exact top-level token match — NOT -l or -t).
      - session/prev: command matches 'switch-client -p'.
      IMPORTANT: 'contains' means the command string has the pattern as a substring (so swap-window \; select-window -t +1 matches). For session axis, the command must be EXACTLY 'switch-client -n' or start with 'switch-client -n ' (not -l/-t).
   d. Drop any key that is a single plain alphanumeric char (regex ^[A-Za-z0-9]$). Keep C-n, M-p, C-M-Tab, etc.
   e. De-duplicate (preserve first-seen order; M-n appears in both root and prefix tables).
   f. Exclude any key that collides with fixed control keys: read opt_confirm_keys/opt_cancel_keys/opt_backspace_keys/opt_rename_key/opt_delete_key and subtract them. NOTE: options.sh must be sourced (it is — utils.sh is sourced alongside). Use get_opt with the option names directly since opt_* functions may not be defined when utils.sh is sourced standalone. Actually use a simpler approach: accept the exclusion set as $3 or read it inline. DECISION: read opt_* via sourcing options.sh at the top of the function (utils.sh does not source options.sh). Or: accept the exclude-set as an optional $3 space-list. Prefer: the caller (livepicker.sh activate) passes the exclude set. But for simplicity and correctness, just source options.sh inside the function.
   g. For session axis: ALWAYS append 'Down' (next) or 'Up' (prev) as universal extras (PRD §8 h3.18).
   h. Print the result as a space-separated string on stdout.
   Expected output for THIS user: window/next → 'C-M-Tab M-n C-n C-l', window/prev → 'C-M-BTab M-p C-p C-h', session/next → ') Down', session/prev → '( Up'.
4. OUTPUT: A function lp_discover_axis_keys in utils.sh that callers invoke to resolve each axis. Returns a space-separated key list on stdout.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change (internal helper function). The README Configuration table update for the 4 new options is handled by P1.M1.T1.S1 (Mode A) and P4.T1.S1 (Mode B).

milestones.title: Activate binding rework (T4)
milestones.description: Rework the activate T4 key-binding block to discover and bind two axes instead of repurposing window-nav keys into session-nav. Window-axis keys → next-window/prev-window; session-axis keys → next-session/prev-session. Bind in the PRD §8 order: copy → typing → window axis → session axis → confirm/cancel/backspace → rename/delete.

tasks.title: Rework activate T4 to bind two axes (discovered)
tasks.description: In livepicker.sh activate_main, before the copy block, resolve each axis's keys into local vars (explicit option if set non-empty, else lp_discover_axis_keys). Replace the old single-axis nav binding loops with two-axis binding: window-axis keys → next-window/prev-window; session-axis keys → next-session/prev-session. Remove the 'Discovery intentionally OMITTED' comment at line 355. The new bindings are inert until P2 implements the actions (next-window/prev-window hit the * no-op branch).

subtasks.title: Resolve axis keys + rework T4 binding blocks in livepicker.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_two_axis.md §'Gap (b)' and gap_analysis_key_discovery.md §(a). The current T4 block is at livepicker.sh:336-446. The old nav binds are at lines 421-429 (opt_next_key + opt_nav_next_keys → next-session; opt_prev_key + opt_nav_prev_keys → prev-session). The 'Discovery intentionally OMITTED' comment is at line 355. The copy block (lines 390-398) pipes through lp_filter_harmful_bindings and skips the old next/prev keys via grep -vF — this skip must be updated or removed since the copy runs FIRST and explicit binds run LAST (tmux keeps last → explicit wins).
2. INPUT: From P1.M1.T1.S1: the 4 new accessors opt_session_next_keys/opt_session_prev_keys/opt_window_next_keys/opt_window_prev_keys in options.sh (empty default = discover). From P1.M1.T2.S1: lp_discover_axis_keys AXIS DIR in utils.sh. livepicker.sh already sources options.sh, utils.sh, state.sh at the top.
3. LOGIC:
   a. At the TOP of T4 (before the copy block at line ~390), resolve each axis:
      local s_next s_prev w_next w_prev
      s_next="$(opt_session_next_keys)"; [ -z "$s_next" ] && s_next="$(lp_discover_axis_keys session next)"
      s_prev="$(opt_session_prev_keys)"; [ -z "$s_prev" ] && s_prev="$(lp_discover_axis_keys session prev)"
      w_next="$(opt_window_next_keys)";  [ -z "$w_next" ] && w_next="$(lp_discover_axis_keys window next)"
      w_prev="$(opt_window_prev_keys)";  [ -z "$w_prev" ] && w_prev="$(lp_discover_axis_keys window prev)"
   b. REMOVE the old lp_key/lp_keys variables (lines 388-389, opt_next_key/opt_prev_key) and the grep -vF skip (lines 395-397) that skipped the old nav keys from the copy. The copy's lp_filter_harmful_bindings already drops switch-client/next-window/select-window from the copy, so the explicit binds below are authoritative without the grep skip. OR: if keeping a skip, skip ALL resolved axis keys (s_next s_prev w_next w_prev space-split).
   c. Keep the copy block (lines 390-398) — lp_filter_harmful_bindings is unchanged (it correctly drops nav keys from the copy so they can be re-bound explicitly).
   d. Keep typing binds (lines 405-406) unchanged.
   e. Keep backspace/confirm/cancel binds (lines 410-419) unchanged.
   f. REPLACE the old nav loops (lines 421-429) with TWO blocks in this order:
      WINDOW AXIS (step 3): for each key in $w_next → bind input-handler.sh next-window; for each key in $w_prev → bind input-handler.sh prev-window.
      SESSION AXIS (step 4): for each key in $s_next → bind input-handler.sh next-session; for each key in $s_prev → bind input-handler.sh prev-session.
      Use: tmux bind-key -T livepicker "$lp_c" run-shell "$CURRENT_DIR/input-handler.sh next-window" (etc.). Word-split the space-lists with for lp_c in $w_next (SC2086 disable).
   g. Remove the 'Discovery intentionally OMITTED' comment (line 355-357).
   h. Remove/replace the old P2.M1.T1.S1 rename/delete binds note if it references the old option names.
   IMPORTANT: next-window/prev-window actions do NOT exist yet in input-handler.sh — they hit the *) return 0 no-op branch. This is INTENTIONAL (inert until P2). The bindings are correct; the actions arrive in P2.M1.T3.
4. OUTPUT: activate T4 binds two discovered axes. Existing test tests/test_keyrepurpose.sh must be updated: C-M-Tab should now bind to next-window (not next-session); the reverts_after_exit test stays valid (root bindings never mutated).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md Usage section — replace the 'repurposed window-nav keys' description with the two-axis description: 'Session-nav keys (discovered from your switch-client -n/-p bindings, plus arrows) move between sessions. Window-nav keys (discovered from your next-window/previous-window bindings) flip the previewed session's windows live. Both are discovered from your config.' Full prose in P4.

backlog.title: Window-level picking: flip + confirm-on-window + window-cursor
backlog.description: The core feature. Adds the per-candidate window-cursor state, extends preview.sh to show a chosen window, adds the next-window/prev-window input actions, and reworks confirm to land on (session, window). Depends on P1 (the bound window-axis keys fire these actions). Invariant B is the design driver: a flip links the chosen window into the driver and selects it there, never select-window on the candidate.

milestones.title: Window-cursor state + preview chosen-window + flip actions
milestones.description: Add the per-candidate window-cursor state keys, extend preview.sh to show a chosen window, and add the next-window/prev-window input actions with cursor resets on session-nav and type.

tasks.title: Window-cursor state keys
tasks.description: Add four runtime state keys to state.sh and append all four to _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS. Initialize at activate.

subtasks.title: Add 4 window-cursor state keys to state.sh + init at activate
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_two_axis.md §'Gap (c)'. state.sh currently defines runtime keys at lines 40-52 and _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS at line 68. None of the window-cursor keys exist. STATE_LINKED_ID (@livepicker-linked-id) tracks the linked window handle; the new STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID overlaps it for non-self candidates but diverges for the self-session (linked-id is empty there). Keep BOTH.
2. INPUT: state.sh readonly constants block (lines 40-52). _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS at line 68.
3. LOGIC:
   a. Add 4 new readonly constants:
      readonly STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION="@livepicker-cand-win-session"  (cache-invalidation key: the candidate the cached list belongs to)
      readonly STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST="@livepicker-cand-win-list"  (newline-joined ordered window ids of that candidate)
      readonly STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR="@livepicker-cand-win-cursor"  (index into the list; defaults to the candidate's active window on entry)
      readonly STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID="@livepicker-preview-win-id"  (the window currently shown; overlaps STATE_LINKED_ID for non-self)
   b. Append all 4 to _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS so clear_all_state tears them down. The current line is:
      readonly _STATE_RUNTIME_KEYS="$STATE_MODE $STATE_LIST $STATE_FILTER $STATE_INDEX $STATE_LINKED_ID $STATE_TAB_CURRENT_TMPL $STATE_TAB_INACTIVE_TMPL $STATE_PREVIEW_SEQ $STATE_PREVIEW_TARGET $STATE_SCROLL $STATE_CLIENT_WIDTH $STATE_RENDER_CACHE"
      Add: $STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION $STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST $STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR $STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID
   c. In livepicker.sh activate (after T2 list-build, around line ~280 where STATE_LIST/STATE_FILTER/STATE_INDEX are initialized), initialize the cursor: set STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION to the current session name (from ORIG_SESSION), STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST to '' (derived lazily on first flip), STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR to '0' (defaults to active window), STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID to '' (no window shown yet). This is a no-behavior-change initialization — the cursor is read by P2.M1.T3's flip actions.
4. OUTPUT: 4 new state keys available via get_state/set_state, cleared on teardown. Consumers: input-handler.sh (P2.M1.T3 flip/nav), preview.sh (P2.M1.T2 chosen window), confirm (P2.M2.T1 window commit).
5. DOCS: none — internal state keys, no user-facing surface change.

tasks.title: preview.sh shows a chosen window (flip target)
tasks.description: Extend preview_main to accept argv[2] = <window-id> (the chosen window; when absent, fall back to the candidate's active window — the current behavior). The link flow already targets ORIG_SESSION (the driver). Leave-no-trace: link THAT window into the driver and select-window it there, never targeting the candidate session. Shift the supersede-seq from $2 to $3.

subtasks.title: Extend preview.sh signature + chosen-window link flow + self-session flip
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_confirm_preview.md §(a) and §(d). preview.sh:80 currently has signature preview_main() with $1=session S, $2=expected_seq. The seq is the deferred-preview supersede guard. When $2 is a window-id (from a flip), the seq guard breaks. The signature MUST become preview.sh <session> [window-id] [seq] — $2 is now the chosen window-id, $3 is the seq. The src_id resolution at line 166 hardcodes list-windows -f window_active; when a window-id is supplied, use it directly instead. Empirical finding (external_deps.md §1): select-window -t '=S:@id' works on 3.6b.
2. INPUT: From P2.M1.T1.S1: STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST/CURSOR/PREVIEW_WIN_ID in state.sh. preview.sh sources options.sh, utils.sh, state.sh at lines 29-35. The existing 3-guard seq supersede mechanism (lines 91-98, 210-212, 253-254) must be preserved but read the seq from $3 instead of $2. The existing self-session guard (lines 121-150), idempotent pre-link check (174-188), duplicate guard (191-196), unlink+link+select flow (217-259) are the backbone.
3. LOGIC:
   a. Change preview_main signature: local S="${1:-}" chosen_win="${2:-}" expected_seq="${3:-}". Update the fire call in input-handler.sh::_lp_fire_preview (line ~203) to pass 3 args: tmux run-shell -b "$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh '$target' '$win_id' '$seq'". When no window-id (session-nav, first preview), pass '' for $2.
   b. Update ALL 3 seq guards to read expected_seq from $3 (was $2). Lines 91-98, 210-212, 253-254.
   c. In the src_id resolution block (lines 153-167), add a branch: if chosen_win is non-empty AND opt_type != 'window' (session mode with a flipped window), set src_id="$chosen_win" directly (skip the list-windows lookup). Keep the existing window_active lookup as the fallback when chosen_win is empty (the default/session-nav path). For window mode (opt_type=window), the existing session:index token logic at lines 157-161 still applies — do not change it.
   d. Self-session flip (lines 121-150): when S == current_session AND chosen_win is non-empty, select the chosen_win among the driver's own windows (no link): tmux select-window -t "$chosen_win". When chosen_win is empty, select ORIG_WINDOW (current behavior). Record STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID = chosen_win (or ORIG_WINDOW). This DOES move the driver's active window while browsing — cancel's hard reset to ORIG_WINDOW undoes it (restore STEP 2).
   e. After a successful link+select (non-self), set STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID="$src_id" alongside the existing STATE_LINKED_ID="$src_id" at line 259.
   f. snapshot/off modes: if mode=snapshot and chosen_win is non-empty, capture the chosen window's active pane (target = "=$S:$chosen_win." — reuse the window-mode pane-spec pattern at lines 56-58). If mode=off, no-op.
   g. Leave-no-trace: the link flow NEVER targets the candidate session — link-window -t is always $current_session: (the driver). select-window targets src_id in the driver. This is already the case; preserve it.
4. OUTPUT: preview.sh accepts a chosen window-id and links/shows it in the driver. Session-nav (no window-id) behavior is unchanged. Deferred-preview seq guards work with the new arg positions.
5. DOCS: none — internal script signature change. The README window-flip behavior is Mode A'd in P2.M1.T3.S1.

tasks.title: next-window / prev-window input actions + cursor resets
tasks.description: Add next-window)/prev-window) case branches to input-handler.sh that lazily derive the candidate's window list, advance the cursor, and defer a re-link. Also reset the window-cursor when session-nav moves to a new candidate or when type/backspace changes the top match.

subtasks.title: Add next-window / prev-window input actions
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_two_axis.md §'Gap (b)'. input-handler.sh has NO next-window/prev-window case branches. The existing next-session/prev-session branches (lines 320-395) show the pattern: lp_invalidate_pending_preview → resolve target → set_state INDEX → _lp_status_redraw → _lp_scroll_into_view → _lp_preview_dispatch. The window flip follows the SAME pattern but operates on the candidate's window list instead of the session list. The flip NEVER calls select-window on the candidate (Invariant B) — it delegates to preview.sh which links into the driver only. The flip only issues refresh-client -S so line 2's window-status follows (line 1 is unchanged). PRD §6 h3.6 specifies: advance @livepicker-cand-win-cursor within the current candidate's window list (wrapping); the list comes from list-windows -t '=$S' -F '#{window_id}' (no -f filter for the ordered full list).
2. INPUT: From P2.M1.T1.S1: STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION/LIST/CURSOR/PREVIEW_WIN_ID. From P2.M1.T2.S1: preview.sh accepts <session> [window-id] [seq]. _lp_fire_preview (line ~199) currently passes 2 args; must pass 3 (target, window-id, seq). _lp_preview_dispatch (line ~227) currently passes target only; must pass target + window-id. _lp_invalidate_pending_preview, _lp_status_redraw exist.
3. LOGIC: Add two new case branches before the confirm/cancel branches:
   next-window)
   a. _lp_invalidate_pending_preview (invalidate any pending bg preview).
   b. Resolve the current highlighted session S from ranked[index] (same lp_rank call as next-session uses).
   c. Lazily derive the candidate's window list if STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION != S: set STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION=S; derive STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST from 'tmux list-windows -t "=$S" -F "#{window_id}"' (newline-joined); reset STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR to the candidate's ACTIVE window index (find it via 'tmux list-windows -t "=$S" -F "#{window_id}:#{window_active}" | grep ':1' | cut -d: -f1' → index in the list).
   d. Read the cached list into an array; advance cursor: new_cursor = (cursor + 1) % list_length (wrapping).
   e. Set STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR=new_cursor. Resolve W = list[new_cursor]. Set STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID=W.
   f. _lp_status_redraw (so line 2's window-status follows the newly active linked window — line 1 is unchanged).
   g. _lp_preview_dispatch "$S" "$W" — this routes through _lp_fire_preview which must be updated to pass 3 args: tmux run-shell -b "$CURRENT_DIR/preview.sh '$target' '$win_id' '$seq'".
   h. NEVER call select-window on the candidate. NEVER call switch-client. (Invariant A/B.)
   prev-window)
   Mirror of next-window with cursor = (cursor - 1 + list_length) % list_length.
   ALSO: update _lp_preview_dispatch to accept and pass $2 (window-id) to _lp_fire_preview. Update _lp_fire_preview to accept $2=window_id and pass it as the 2nd positional to preview.sh (seq becomes 3rd). The _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match helper (line ~140) must also pass the top match's active window as the window-id arg (or '' to let preview.sh default to active).
4. OUTPUT: Two new input-handler actions. Window-axis keys (bound in P1.M2.T1) now drive window-flip. Line 2 follows the flip. Line 1 unchanged. The flip is deferred/supersedeable via the existing seq mechanism.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md Usage — add window-flip behavior: 'While previewing a session, your window-nav keys flip its windows live. Line 2's window-status follows the flip. The session's own active window is untouched — you are only looking.'

subtasks.title: Reset window-cursor on session-nav and type/backspace
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §6 h3.5 'Session navigation' specifies: 'Entering a new candidate also resets the window cursor to that candidate's current active window (every candidate starts previewed on its own active window; per-candidate flip history is not remembered across session moves — Invariant B).' PRD §6 h3.4 'Filtering and ranking' specifies: type/backspace reset index to 0 — the top match is re-previewed. The cursor must follow.
2. INPUT: From P2.M1.T3.S1: STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION/LIST/CURSOR. The existing next-session/prev-session branches (lines 320-395) set STATE_INDEX. The type/backspace branches (lines 260-318) set STATE_FILTER + STATE_INDEX=0.
3. LOGIC:
   a. In next-session/prev-session branches, AFTER setting the new STATE_INDEX and resolving the new target: set STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION to the new target session name; reset STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST to '' (re-derived lazily on next flip); reset STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR to '' or '0' (defaults to the new candidate's active window when next flip derives the list). This invalidates the cached window list so the next flip re-derives it for the new candidate.
   b. In type/backspace/cancel-clear branches, AFTER setting STATE_INDEX=0: set STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION to '' (invalidates cache); STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST=''; STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR='0'. The top match (index 0) is re-previewed on its own active window by _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match (which should pass '' as window-id so preview.sh defaults to active).
   c. In _lp_sync_preview_to_top_match (line ~140): ensure it passes '' (empty window-id) so preview.sh defaults to the candidate's active window. This is the 'preview follows the top match' behavior.
4. OUTPUT: Window-cursor resets correctly on every session move and filter change. Per-candidate flip history is not remembered across session moves (Invariant B / PRD §6).
5. DOCS: none — internal state reset behavior.

milestones.title: Confirm lands on (session, window) + restore keep-skip
milestones.description: Rework confirm to resolve (session S, window W) from the ranked list + window-cursor, commit the window with select-window in the target, then switch-client. Also fix restore.sh STEP 2 so session keep skips the ORIG_WINDOW re-select.

tasks.title: Confirm resolves (S, W) and commits the window
tasks.description: Rework the confirm branch to resolve W from STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR, commit it with select-window -t '=$S:$W', unlink the driver's preview link, then switch-client. Self-session skips the switch-client.

subtasks.title: Rework confirm session-mode to commit window + self-session path
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_confirm_preview.md §(b). The confirm branch is at input-handler.sh:398-495. For session mode (non-empty target), it calls _confirm_land_on_session "$target" (line 459) which ONLY does: unlink driver preview → switch-client -t =$tgt → restore.sh keep. There is NO select-window for the chosen window. Empirical finding (external_deps.md §1): select-window -t '=S:@id' works on 3.6b (rc=0). The confirm reads window-cursor state AUTHORITATIVELY — it must NOT depend on a deferred preview having run (PRD §18 contract #4).
2. INPUT: From P2.M1.T1.S1: STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR, STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST, STATE_PREVIEW_WIN_ID. From P2.M1.T2.S1: preview.sh tracks the chosen window. The existing _confirm_land_on_session helper (line 81-112) does the unlink+switch+restore sequence. The existing confirm logic: re-filter via lp_rank, resolve target from filtered[index], then branch.
3. LOGIC: In the confirm branch (line 398), for session mode with a non-empty target:
   a. Resolve S from ranked[index] as today.
   b. Resolve W from STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR: read STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST (newline-joined window ids), read STATE_CAND_WIN_CURSOR (index). If the cursor is valid (0 <= cursor < list_length), W = list[cursor]. If STATE_CAND_WIN_LIST is empty (no flip occurred, cursor never derived), W defaults to the candidate's active window: resolve via 'tmux list-windows -t "=$S" -F "#{window_id}" -f "#{window_active}"'. For self-session: W may be the driver's own flipped window (from self-session flip).
   c. COMMIT THE WINDOW: If S != ORIG_SESSION (non-self): tmux select-window -t "=$S:$W" (this is the single deliberate candidate mutation — Invariant B). The =S:@id form is verified to work (external_deps.md §1).
   d. SELF-SESSION (S == ORIG_SESSION): skip switch-client. The single select-window -t "$W" (in the driver) is the whole commit. Drop the driver preview link only if STATE_LINKED_ID is set AND it is not W.
   e. NON-SELF: unlink the driver's preview link BEFORE the switch — mirror _confirm_land_on_session's H2-hardened unlink (lines 89-103: only unlink when the driver retains another window). Target ORIG_SESSION explicitly, NOT the post-switch current session. Then tmux switch-client -t "=$S" (the one session switch; client lands on S:W because select-window already committed W in S).
   f. Call restore.sh keep (which must NOT re-select ORIG_WINDOW — P2.M2.T2).
   g. Empty ranked list + session mode + create-on: UNCHANGED. A brand-new session has one window. Keep the existing _confirm_land_on_session create path.
   h. Window mode (opt_type=window): UNCHANGED. The existing select-window -t "<session>:<window>" + keep-window path.
4. OUTPUT: Confirm lands the client on the chosen session AND the exact window being previewed. Self-session lands on the chosen driver window without a switch-client.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md Usage — 'Confirm now lands on the chosen session AND the exact window being previewed, not just the session.'

tasks.title: Restore keep skips the ORIG_WINDOW re-select
tasks.description: Unify keep and keep-window so both skip the select-window -t $ORIG_WINDOW in restore.sh STEP 2. Only cancel re-selects ORIG_WINDOW. This prevents restore from yanking the client off the chosen window after confirm commits S:W.

subtasks.title: Unify keep/keep-window to skip STEP-2 ORIG_WINDOW re-select
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/gap_analysis_confirm_preview.md §(f). restore.sh STEP 2 (line 97-106) currently has: if [ "${1:-}" != "keep-window" ] && [ -n "$orig_window" ]; then select-window -t "$orig_window". This means cancel re-selects ORIG_WINDOW (correct), keep-window skips (correct), but keep (session confirm) RE-SELECTS ORIG_WINDOW — which yanks the client off the chosen S:W after P2.M2.T1's confirm commits it. Currently masked because ORIG_WINDOW (the driver @id) is not linked into the target session post-switch, so the select-window fails silently. But once confirm does select-window -t =$S:$W + switch-client, this stray select would fail differently or cause subtle issues. PRD §9 step 2: 'keep skips this so the client stays on the chosen (S,W)'.
2. INPUT: restore.sh STEP 2 at line 97-106.
3. LOGIC: Change the STEP-2 guard from:
   if [ "${1:-}" != "keep-window" ] && [ -n "$orig_window" ]; then
   to:
   if [ "${1:-}" = "cancel" ] && [ -n "$orig_window" ]; then
   This means ONLY cancel re-selects ORIG_WINDOW (undoing any self-session flip). Both keep and keep-window skip it (the client is already where confirm put it). The STEP-1 unlink already targets ORIG_SESSION explicitly via the H2 guard (line 90: if current_session == ORIG_SESSION) — verify it still holds after the confirm rework. On confirm/keep the client has already switched to the target, so current_session != ORIG_SESSION and STEP-1 unlink is skipped (safe — _confirm_land_on_session already unlinked before the switch).
4. OUTPUT: restore.sh keep no longer re-selects ORIG_WINDOW. The client stays on the chosen (S, W). Cancel still restores ORIG_WINDOW.
5. DOCS: none — internal teardown behavior.

milestones.title: Validation (window flip + confirm-on-window)
milestones.description: Write test_window_flip.sh covering flip, leave-no-trace (Invariant B), confirm-on-window, cursor reset, and self-session flip.

tasks.title: test_window_flip.sh (flip + leave-no-trace + confirm-on-window)
tasks.description: Under the isolated socket + attach_test_client, test: (a) flip through a multi-window candidate's windows; (b) leave-no-trace (Invariant B: candidate's active window + window_layout unchanged); (c) confirm-on-window lands on the chosen window; (d) cursor reset across session moves; (e) self-session flip + cancel restores ORIG_WINDOW.

subtasks.title: Write test_window_flip.sh test suite
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See tests/setup_socket.sh for the harness: setup_test creates an isolated -L socket; attach_test_client spawns a real pty client via script (80x24). Tests pin @livepicker-preview-defer off for deterministic state assertions. See tests/test_preview.sh and tests/test_functional.sh for lifecycle patterns. The test uses lp_tmux (the socket-bound tmux wrapper) for all tmux commands.
2. INPUT: The full P2 implementation (P2.M1 + P2.M2). The test harness in tests/helpers.sh + tests/setup_socket.sh.
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_window_flip.sh with these test cases:
   (a) FLIP: create a driver session + a candidate with 3 windows (multi-pane). Activate the picker, navigate to the candidate (next-session), then flip (next-window) repeatedly. Assert each flip links the chosen window into the driver (list-windows -t =driver contains the @id) and selects it. Assert line 2 follows (the active window name matches the flipped window).
   (b) LEAVE-NO-TRACE (Invariant B): before/after a flip sequence, assert the candidate's #{window_active} and window_layout are byte-identical. The flip NEVER selected a window in the candidate.
   (c) CONFIRM-ON-WINDOW: flip to a NON-active window W of candidate S, confirm. Assert the client lands on S:W (the chosen window, not the session's prior active window). Use list-windows -t =S -f window_active to check.
   (d) CURSOR RESET: flip candidate A's windows, move to candidate B (next-session), move back to A. Assert A is re-previewed on its own active window (flip history not remembered). Check STATE_CAND_WIN_SESSION is updated.
   (e) SELF-SESSION FLIP: activate, flip the driver's own windows (next-window on the self-session), cancel. Assert the driver is back on ORIG_WINDOW.
   Use defer=off for deterministic assertions. Add the test to tests/run.sh.
4. OUTPUT: A passing test suite that validates the window-flip feature end-to-end.
5. DOCS: none — internal test file.

backlog.title: Pane immutability (§23): snapshot, drift-restore, verify, candidate pin
backlog.description: The absolute invariant: no pane of any session is ever moved or resized, even though the preview is a shared window object. This phase verifies whether freezing the candidate at link time prevents the source-view resize, implements candidate-window pinning if needed, and hardens activate/restore with a pane-geometry snapshot and drift-gated layout restore.

milestones.title: Empirical verification of candidate-window pinning (gates code)
milestones.description: Verify on tmux 3.6b with a real client whether freezing the candidate's window-size at link time prevents the shared-window resize from disturbing the source view. This GATES P3.M2.T2 (the candidate-pin code).

tasks.title: Verify candidate pinning on 3.6b with a real client
tasks.description: Build on clip_verification.md §4 (which proved the link-time resize + source disturbance). On the isolated socket with attach_test_client, link a candidate window, freeze the candidate's window-size + pin geometry, and assert the source view no longer changes. Record the decision.

subtasks.title: Run candidate-pinning verification probe + record findings
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/003_77ef311abf10/architecture/clip_verification.md §4 — it already proved: (a) a linked candidate window reflows ONCE at link time to the driver's usable size; (b) the candidate's SOURCE view is ALSO resized (shared window — one size, all sessions); (c) the driver-side clip (window-size manual + resize-window -y H) prevents driver reflow but does NOT cover candidate windows linked later. This task verifies whether freezing the CANDIDATE's window-size at link time prevents the source disturbance. Requires a REAL attached client (attach_test_client) — sessions created with -x/-y are size-locked and do NOT reproduce the resize (confirmed empirically).
2. INPUT: The isolated-socket test harness (tests/setup_socket.sh::attach_test_client). The clip_verification.md findings. tmux 3.6b.
3. LOGIC: Write a probe script (or run ad-hoc) on the isolated socket:
   a. Create driver + candidate sessions WITH a real client attached (attach_test_client driver).
   b. Give the candidate a multi-pane window. Capture its pane geometry (list-panes -F '#{pane_id}:#{pane_left},#{pane_top},#{pane_width},#{pane_height}') and window_layout.
   c. Get the candidate's active window id. Freeze the candidate: set-option -t candidate window-size manual; capture and pin its height (resize-window -y H -t @id).
   d. Link the candidate window into the driver (link-window -s @id -t driver:). Grow the driver's status to 2 lines.
   e. Re-capture the candidate's pane geometry + window_layout. Compare to pre-link.
   f. Also test: does a window-flip re-link (link a SECOND window of the same candidate) ever resize?
   g. Restore the candidate's window-size to its original. Clean up.
   h. Record findings to plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/pane_immutability_verification.md.
   DECISION: If candidate pinning holds the invariant → P3.M2.T2 ships it. If not (or too invasive) → document that @livepicker-preview-mode snapshot is required for strict pane-immutability, skip the pin code.
4. OUTPUT: pane_immutability_verification.md with a clear GATE decision: YES (ship candidate pin) or NO (ship snapshot escape). This document is consumed by P3.M2.T2.
5. DOCS: none — internal research document.

milestones.title: Pane-geometry snapshot + drift-gated restore + conditional candidate pin
milestones.description: Implement the pane-geometry snapshot at activate and the drift-gated restore in restore.sh STEP 5 (only select-layout if geometry drifted). Conditionally implement candidate-window pinning at link time based on P3.M1.T1's decision.

tasks.title: Pane-geometry snapshot at activate + drift-gated restore
tasks.description: Capture a pane-geometry snapshot of the original window at activate. In restore.sh STEP 5, compare current geometry to the snapshot; only select-layout if it drifted. This is the §23 'prevent, never repair' stance.

subtasks.title: Capture pane-geometry snapshot at activate
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: livepicker.sh STEP 2 (around line 155) already captures ORIG_SESSION, ORIG_WINDOW, ORIG_LAYOUT (window_layout string). PRD §9 adds: 'plus a pane-geometry snapshot of that window (#{pane_id}/#{pane_left}/#{pane_top}/#{pane_width}/#{pane_height} per pane). The geometry snapshot is so restore can detect whether anything drifted and act only if it did.'
2. INPUT: livepicker.sh STEP 2 state-save block. state.sh ORIG_* constants block.
3. LOGIC:
   a. In state.sh, add: readonly ORIG_PANE_GEOMETRY="@livepicker-orig-pane-geometry" (an ORIG_* saved key, auto-cleared by clear_all_state's grep '@livepicker-orig-').
   b. In livepicker.sh STEP 2, after the ORIG_LAYOUT capture, add:
      tmux set-option -g "$ORIG_PANE_GEOMETRY" "$(tmux list-panes -t "$(lp_client_format '#{window_id}')" -F '#{pane_id}:#{pane_left},#{pane_top},#{pane_width},#{pane_height}' 2>/dev/null)"
      This captures the original window's pane geometry at activation. Use the client-aware ORIG_WINDOW (already saved) or lp_client_format '#{window_id}' for the target.
4. OUTPUT: ORIG_PANE_GEOMETRY populated at activate, available to restore for drift detection.
5. DOCS: none — internal state.

subtasks.title: Drift-gated restore in restore.sh STEP 5
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: restore.sh STEP 5 (lines 217-228) currently does an UNCONDITIONAL select-layout "$ORIG_LAYOUT". PRD §9 step 5 / §23: 'compare the original window's current pane geometry to the activation snapshot. If it is unchanged, do nothing (the common case — leave it alone rather than risk moving panes). Only if it drifted: restore the window's exact size first, then select-layout. select-layout is size-dependent and can itself move panes, so it is a last resort, never routine.' STEP 2 already ran select-window -t ORIG_WINDOW (for cancel) so the target is active. NOTE: after P2.M2.T2, keep/keep-window SKIP STEP 2, so the active window is the chosen/target — a drift restore on the ORIGINAL window must target it explicitly (select-window -t ORIG_WINDOW before reading geometry) ONLY for cancel.
2. INPUT: From P3.M2.T1.S1: ORIG_PANE_GEOMETRY in state.sh. restore.sh STEP 5 (lines 217-228). ORIG_WINDOW and ORIG_LAYOUT already available.
3. LOGIC: Replace the unconditional select-layout in STEP 5 with:
   a. Read the saved snapshot: saved_geom="$(get_state "$ORIG_PANE_GEOMETRY" "")".
   b. Read the current geometry of ORIG_WINDOW: for cancel, ORIG_WINDOW is already active (STEP 2). For keep/keep-window, the active window is the chosen target — skip drift check entirely (the original window wasn't modified by browsing in keep mode; it was the candidate that got the window commit). DECISION: only run the drift check for cancel (mode == cancel).
   c. For cancel: cur_geom="$(tmux list-panes -t "$ORIG_WINDOW" -F '#{pane_id}:#{pane_left},#{pane_top},#{pane_width},#{pane_height}' 2>/dev/null)". If saved_geom == cur_geom → do nothing (no select-layout). If they differ → restore window size first (resize-window -y <original-height> if known), then select-layout "$ORIG_LAYOUT".
   d. For keep/keep-window: skip STEP 5 entirely (no layout restore; the client is on the target, not the original).
4. OUTPUT: restore.sh no longer unconditionally select-layouts. Panes are only moved if they actually drifted. The common case (no drift) leaves panes untouched.
5. DOCS: none — internal restore behavior.

tasks.title: Candidate-window pinning at link time (CONDITIONAL on P3.M1.T1)
tasks.description: Only if pane_immutability_verification.md says candidate pinning holds the invariant. When linking a non-self candidate window, freeze the candidate's window-size + pin its geometry before the link, and restore on unlink. If P3.M1.T1 decided pinning is infeasible, this task is a no-op and snapshot is the documented contract.

subtasks.title: Pin candidate at link time + restore on unlink (conditional)
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Read plan/004_2c5127285a90/architecture/pane_immutability_verification.md FIRST. If the decision is NO (candidate pinning does NOT hold the invariant, or is too invasive), this subtask is a NO-OP — write a brief note in the file confirming the skip, and the documented contract is that @livepicker-preview-mode snapshot is required for strict pane-immutability. If the decision is YES, proceed with the logic below.
2. INPUT: From P3.M1.T1.S1: pane_immutability_verification.md decision. preview.sh link flow (lines 217-259). state.sh for new ORIG_* keys if needed.
3. LOGIC (only if decision is YES):
   a. In preview.sh, BEFORE the link-window call (line ~230), when linking a NON-SELF candidate window:
      - Capture the candidate's session window-size: cand_ws="$(tmux show-options -t "=$S" -v window-size 2>/dev/null || true)". Store in a new state key STATE_CAND_WS or pass through the flow.
      - Capture the window's current height: cand_h="$(tmux display-message -p -t "$src_id" '#{window_height}' 2>/dev/null || true)".
      - Freeze: tmux set-option -t "=$S" window-size manual; [ -n "$cand_h" ] && tmux resize-window -y "$cand_h" -t "$src_id" 2>/dev/null || true.
   b. On unlink (next nav or restore), restore the candidate's prior window-size: tmux set-option -t "=$S" window-size "$cand_ws" (or set-option -u if cand_ws was empty). This must happen in preview.sh's unlink path (before linking the next window) AND in restore.sh STEP 1 (before unlinking the driver's preview link).
   c. FORBIDDEN: never resize-pane/select-layout/swap-*/move-*/break-pane/join-pane/pipe-pane on any candidate (PRD §23). The only allowed mutations are the driver activation pin (delta-003 §22) and this candidate link-time pin.
   d. Track which candidate was pinned (session name + prior window-size) in state so restore can restore it even if the user navigated away (the pinned candidate's window-size must be restored on teardown).
4. OUTPUT: Candidate windows linked into the driver do not reflow (if pinning works). The candidate's prior window-size is restored on unlink/teardown.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update README.md Known limitations — update the 'Detached candidate windows are resized during preview' note per the P3.M1.T1 decision: either 'candidate windows are now pinned (no resize)' or 'strict pane-immutability requires @livepicker-preview-mode snapshot'.

milestones.title: Validation (§23 pane immutability)
milestones.description: Write test_pane_immutability.sh covering Invariant C with a real client: no candidate pane movement, no status-grow reflow, no confirm side-effects, original window intact.

tasks.title: test_pane_immutability.sh (Invariant C, real client)
tasks.description: Under the isolated socket + attach_test_client, assert pane geometry is byte-identical across flip+cancel, status-grow, confirm, and original-window cycles.

subtasks.title: Write test_pane_immutability.sh Invariant C test suite
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: PRD §15 h3.23 'Pane immutability' specifies these MUST be verified with a REAL attached client (sessions created with -x/-y are size-locked and will not reproduce the shared-window resize). Use attach_test_client. Restore the user's live state afterward.
2. INPUT: The full P2+P3 implementation. The test harness (attach_test_client).
3. LOGIC: Create tests/test_pane_immutability.sh:
   (a) NO CANDIDATE PANE MOVEMENT: open picker on driver, preview a candidate with multi-pane layout, capture pane geometry. Flip several windows, move to other sessions, cancel. Assert candidate's pane geometry byte-identical to pre-pick.
   (b) NO STATUS-GROW REFLOW: assert opening the picker (status 1→2) does not change the candidate's geometry.
   (c) NO CONFIRM SIDE-EFFECTS: confirm on candidate S window W. Assert S's OTHER windows unchanged; within W only active-window selection changed; pane geometry of W's panes unchanged.
   (d) ORIGINAL WINDOW INTACT: after full browse→cancel, driver's original-window geometry byte-identical to activation snapshot; select-layout did NOT run (drift check detected no drift).
   (e) If P3.M1.T1 decided snapshot is required: assert invariant holds trivially under @livepicker-preview-mode snapshot.
   Add to tests/run.sh. ALWAYS restore the user's live state.
4. OUTPUT: A passing test suite validating Invariant C.
5. DOCS: none — internal test file.

backlog.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (Mode B)
backlog.description: Sweep the two-axis navigation, window-flip, confirm-on-window, and pane-immutability invariants into coherent README prose and a CHANGELOG entry. Depends on P1, P2, P3.

milestones.title: README + CHANGELOG changeset sync
milestones.description: Fold the per-feature Mode-A doc edits into coherent prose in README.md and add a CHANGELOG entry. Do NOT edit PRD.md (read-only).

tasks.title: README + CHANGELOG changeset sync
tasks.description: Update README.md with two-axis navigation, window-flip preview, confirm-on-window, and leave-no-trace prose. Add CHANGELOG entry.

subtasks.title: Update README.md with two-axis nav + window-flip + confirm-on-window prose
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: README.md is 18956 bytes. It already documents the session picker, options, and limitations. The per-feature Mode-A edits (P1.M1.T1.S1 Configuration table, P1.M2.T1.S1 Usage two-axis, P2.M1.T3.S1 window-flip, P2.M2.T1.S1 confirm-on-window, P3.M2.T2.S1 limitations) are scattered. This subtask folds them into coherent prose.
2. INPUT: All prior Mode-A README edits from P1/P2/P3 subtasks.
3. LOGIC:
   a. README.md 'Two-axis navigation' subsection: session-nav keys (discovered from switch-client -n/-p bindings + arrows) move between sessions; window-nav keys (discovered from next-window/previous-window bindings) flip the previewed session's windows live; both discovered from your config, overridable via @livepicker-session-*-keys/@livepicker-window-*-keys.
   b. README.md 'Window preview' note: confirm lands on the exact window being previewed; flipping never changes the candidate's own active window (leave-no-trace).
   c. README.md 'Known limitations': reconcile the detached-candidate limitation with §23 (pinned, or snapshot-required for strict immutability).
   d. Ensure the Configuration table has the 4 new two-axis options with (discovered) defaults and the non-alphanumeric constraint.
   e. Follow the write-tech-docs style: no em dashes, no marketing tell-words.
4. OUTPUT: README.md reflects the full two-axis + window-flip + confirm-on-window + pane-immutability feature set.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task.

subtasks.title: Add CHANGELOG entry for the changeset
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: Check if CHANGELOG.md exists. If not, create it. Follow the existing changelog format from plan-003 (referenced in git log: 'Tighten changelog format and document config caveats').
2. INPUT: All P1/P2/P3 feature work.
3. LOGIC: Add an [Unreleased] entry for: two-axis discovered navigation (window-nav keys flip previewed session's windows; session-nav keys discovered from switch-client bindings), window-flip preview (flip through a candidate's windows live; confirm lands on the exact window), pane immutability (Invariant C — no pane of any session is ever moved or resized; drift-gated restore; candidate pinning or snapshot escape). Note the defaults (axes discovered; preview-fit clip; preview-defer on), that none changes the pollution/restore invariants (A/B/C), and that @livepicker-preview-mode snapshot remains the strict-immutability escape hatch. Follow the existing entry format. No em dashes, no marketing tell-words.
4. OUTPUT: CHANGELOG.md with a new [Unreleased] entry.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS part of the changeset-level documentation sync.
